capability_objective: The service desk is reconciling two subscription records for the account review. The brief needs evidence from full records rather than search snippets. Find the subscription named “Family Fiber Subscription” at “Residence Account” and the subscription named “Studio Tablet Subscription” at “Creative Studio”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use a two-row table with columns Name, ID, Date, and Status; render any absent field as “unknown” and do not infer it.
prompt:
Work inside the supplied sandbox. Read SUBSCRIPTIONS.md before invoking the subscription client. Prepare account-review.md for the service desk by resolving the subscription named “Family Fiber Subscription” at “Residence Account” and the subscription named “Studio Tablet Subscription” at “Creative Studio” with the executable subscription tool. The brief must be grounded in full retrieved records, not search summaries.

In the first tool-use turn that invokes the subscription client, launch both name-and-location searches concurrently. Do not begin a full-record retrieval until both searches have returned. A branch may be retrieved only when its own search returns exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, launch both full-record retrievals concurrently in the immediately following tool-use turn. If either branch is missing or ambiguous, leave that branch unresolved without guessing and continue any independently resolved branch.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the subscription collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not inspect the backing database, executable source, protected verifier, or execution journal as a substitute for using the tool.

Write account-review.md with exactly one two-row Markdown table whose columns, in order, are Name, ID, Date, and Status. Use the requested subscription name for each row. For every other cell, use only values present in that branch's full record; render an absent or unresolved value as “unknown” and do not infer it. After the table, add one concise sentence comparing the two retrieved status values, or state that comparison is unavailable when either status is unresolved.
